pattern: Tagging every node with its (column, row) coordinate and sorting by column, then row, then value produces the fully deterministic top-to-bottom, left-to-right vertical grouping.
hints: Give the root column zero; a left child shifts column down by one and a right child up by one, while every child's row is one deeper.
Collect a triple (column, row, value) for each node, because ties within a column are broken first by row and then by value.
Traverse recording (col, row, val) for each node, sort all triples by that tuple, then group consecutive equal columns into the output lists of values.

edge_inputs.description: two nodes sharing a column ordered top to bottom
perfect tree with three nodes in the center column
same coordinate nodes broken by value
two nodes at same position differ in value order

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes and possible value collisions
stress.note: small value range forces frequent same-coordinate value tie-breaks

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def verticalTraversal(self, root):
        from collections import defaultdict
        cols = defaultdict(list)
        stack = [(root, 0, 0)]
        while stack:
            node, r, c = stack.pop()
            if node is None:
                continue
            cols[c].append((r, node.val))
            stack.append((node.left, r + 1, c - 1))
            stack.append((node.right, r + 1, c + 1))
        res = []
        for c in sorted(cols):
            entries = sorted(cols[c])
            res.append([v for _, v in entries])
        return res
